title: The Wiener–Khinchin Theorem and Power Spectral Density
description: Deriving the power spectral density as the Fourier transform of the autocorrelation function, and extending it to cross-correlation and cross-spectral density.
objectives: Derive the Wiener–Khinchin theorem and state the key properties (real, even, non-negative) of the resulting power spectral density
Interpret the PSD as power per unit frequency, and apply the filtering relation S_Y(f) = |H(f)|^2 S_X(f)
Define cross-correlation and cross-spectral density, and use them to estimate a time delay between two related signals
Verify numerically, from a finite noisy realization, that a directly-computed autocorrelation matches the periodogram-based estimate
